qa_pairs.question: What does the `make` command do when it is called?
qa_pairs.answer: When the `make` command is called, it verifies the dates of the various components from which your program is built.

qa_pairs.question: Which routines are recompiled by the `make` command?
qa_pairs.answer: Only routines that were modified after the last compilation of the program are recompiled into object form (files ending in .o).

qa_pairs.question: What happens to the recompiled object files (files ending in .o)?
qa_pairs.answer: Recompiled .o files are subsequently linked together to form an updated version of the final program.

qa_pairs.question: How do you adapt this script to your program?
qa_pairs.answer: To adapt this script to your program, you need to modify the contents of the variables within it.

qa_pairs.question: What provides guidance when modifying the variables to adapt the script?
qa_pairs.answer: Comments within the script will guide you on how and where to modify the variables.

qa_pairs.question: What is the typical file extension for recompiled object files?
qa_pairs.answer: Recompiled object files typically have the .o extension.
